Profile snapshot

Tagline & Bio
"Senior backend engineer building distributed systems for the African fintech wave." Eight years building payment infrastructure across Lagos, Cape Town, and Berlin. Specialized in event-driven architectures, Postgres at scale, and Go services that don't crash at 3 AM. Currently focused on idempotent transaction systems and the boring-but-critical work that keeps money moving.

Public reviews · 4.9 avg from 12 hires
★★★★★ Studio Berlin GmbH · Apr 2026
Built our payment reconciliation pipeline from zero. Hit deadlines, wrote tests, and explained tradeoffs in plain language during architecture reviews. Would hire again.
★★★★★ Quantum Robotics · Apr 2026
Senior eng you can throw a vague problem at and get back something thoughtful. Caught an idempotency bug in our existing code while building the new feature.

03 · 08

Profile snapshot

About · public-facing
"An independent brand & identity studio in Berlin. We work with companies that build interesting things — most of them, you've heard of." Founded 2018 by two former Pentagram designers. Studio Berlin specializes in identity systems, packaging, and digital product design for fintech, hospitality, and consumer-tech clients across Europe and North America. Notable past work: rebrand of Trade Republic (2021), identity for the Mast restaurant group (2023).
Company details
Industry
Design · Brand & Identity
Headquarters
Berlin, Germany
Company size
12–50 employees
Founded
March 2018
Hiring focus
Engineering, design, and design-engineering hybrids
Repeat hire rate
62% (above platform average of 38%)
Categories hired for
Senior Engineer Brand Designer Frontend Engineer Data Analyst Product Designer Motion Designer
Trust tier earned
Top Client
Earned after 15 successful hires with zero unresolved disputes. Top Client status grants priority shortlists, lower fees, and dedicated specialist attention.
Public reviews from talent · 4.8 avg from 23 hires
★★★★★ Adesuwa Babatunde · Apr 2026
Clear scope, fair rate, fast feedback cycles. Lukas knows what he's doing technically — code reviews are useful, not performative. Would work with again.
★★★★★ Anika Patel · Mar 2026
Best client engagement I've had on Atlas. They paid early once because their accounting was running ahead of schedule. Real respect for craft.

Notes

Last updated 2d ago
Manager's notes Mateo Vargas Manager + Admin

"Daniel is the team's most consistent operator. Worth coaching toward team-lead-style responsibilities — he's already mentoring Lukas C. informally."

Q1 highlights from 1:1s:

  • Wants more exposure to dispute mediation training (signed up for May course)
  • Has expressed interest in eventually moving into recruitment-sprint coordination
  • Caseload above target — discussed with Mateo on Apr 28; rebalancing 5 cases to Lukas C.
  • Excellent rapport with Studio Berlin and other Berlin-area clients · "use this as a model"
Last edited Apr 28, 2026 11:42 AM by Mateo Vargas
Admin's notes Aïsha Okafor (you) Admin-only

HR-side observations from Mar 2026 quarterly check:

  • Compliance training fully current (last completed Apr 5)
  • No HR-flagged behaviors; no peer complaints
  • Manager flagged "consistently above caseload target" — confirmed acceptable as long as SLA holds (it does)
  • Eligible for retention review at end of Q2 per the 3-year tenure milestone
Last edited Mar 12, 2026 by Aïsha Okafor (you)

Manager · Mateo Vargas

05 · 08

Notes

Last updated 4d ago
Admin's notes Aïsha Okafor (you) Admin-only

"Mateo is the most consistent operator on the platform. Five years running, never missed a daily report, never failed an audit cycle. His judgement on caseload and dispute escalations is uniquely well-calibrated."

Q1 observations from quarterly Admin review:

  • Calibration variance with my own ratings was ±0.4 — within excellent band
  • Handles his own personal caseload at 62% capacity, leaving headroom for managerial work — good split
  • Cross-team coordination on the Customer Support pool depletion was textbook — escalated cleanly with full data + proposal attached
  • Watch: he's overdue for an exec-track conversation. Tenure milestone Q3 2026.
Last edited Apr 26, 2026 by Aïsha Okafor (you)
Self-evaluation Mateo Vargas Self + Admin

"My calibration with Admin is tighter this quarter than last. Two areas to keep working on: (1) anticipating pool depletion earlier — Customer Support shouldn't have surprised me, the trend was visible 30 days out, (2) more deliberate succession planning — Daniel is ready for more, I should be giving him concrete stretches."

Submitted as part of Q1 2026 self-evaluation cycle.

Submitted Apr 10, 2026 · auto-shared with Admin Read-only · self-eval window closed

Performance review history

5 reviews on file · all delivered by Admin
Q1 2026 reviewLatest Final · delivered Apr 14, 2026 · cycle annual
Overall rating Exceeds expectations ★★★★★ Audit score 4.6 / 5
Admin · Aïsha Okafor (you)
Five years of remarkable consistency. Mateo's calibration with my own ratings is the tightest on the platform. The Customer Support pool intervention this quarter was textbook escalation — clean data, clear proposal, executed without friction. One growth area: more proactive talent development for his strongest specialists, particularly Daniel.
Super Admin · Dario Mensah
Mateo is one of the platform's most strategic assets. His judgement on edge cases — particularly cross-category coordination — is uniquely good. Recommend formal exec-track conversation by end of Q3. He's ready, and the platform needs people of his caliber moving up.

JOB ACTIONS
01 · 05

Job description

Posted Apr 26 · edited Apr 29 (rate range tightened)
Full posting · client-facing copy
412 words · 2-min read · auto-saved Apr 29 17:42 CET

About the role

Studio Berlin is hiring a Senior Frontend Engineer to lead the rebuild of our client-facing portfolio platform — a Vue 3 / Nuxt 3 SPA with a custom design system, hand-built motion, and a small but opinionated codebase. You'll work directly with our founder Stefan Müller and our backend engineer Adesuwa.

This is a long-term, ongoing engagement (3-month minimum, open-ended after) at 25–30 hours/week. We're flexible on timezone but expect ~4 hours of overlap with Berlin (CET) for sync work.

What you'll do

  • Lead the migration from Vue 2 / Nuxt 2 to Vue 3 / Nuxt 3 (in progress, ~30% complete)
  • Build and maintain a small but precise design system in Vue + Tailwind
  • Collaborate with Adesuwa on the BFF layer (Hono / Drizzle on Cloudflare Workers)
  • Own performance: target Lighthouse ≥95, LCP <1.5s on 4G, zero CLS regressions
  • Ship motion and interaction polish — subtle but considered (think Linear, not stripe.com)

What we're looking for

  • 5+ years of professional frontend experience, with at least 2 years on Vue 3 or Nuxt 3
  • Strong sensibility for editorial / brand-led design — you can take an unfinished comp and finish it
  • Comfort with TypeScript, Tailwind, and modern build tooling (Vite)
  • Experience with motion libraries (GSAP, Motion One, or hand-crafted)
  • Async-friendly · clear writing · self-directed

Nice to have

  • Background in agency or studio work (vs. pure product)
  • Experience with Cloudflare Workers / edge compute
  • A portfolio with at least one project you're genuinely proud of (we'll ask)

Compensation & logistics

Hourly rate
$55–65/h
based on experience · weekly cycle
Hours
25–30/wk
~4h CET overlap required
Commitment
3-month min
open-ended after · 30-day notice

About Studio Berlin

We're a 4-person industrial design and brand studio in Berlin (real legal name: Atelier Werkraum GmbH — Studio Berlin is our trading name). We work with consumer hardware companies, exhibition designers, and a small handful of long-term retainer clients. Our existing engineering team is one full-time backend (Adesuwa, also through Atlas) and Stefan dabbles in code. Quiet, considered, no rush culture.

Specialist deciding Stage 3 · 1 day to SLA opened Apr 24, 2026 · 6d 14h elapsed

Work quality dispute on a fixed-price DevOps engagement

SB Studio Berlin REAL: ATELIER WERKRAUM VS TO Tomás Oliveira · eng-004 · $8,500 fixed-price · $4,250 escrow held
DSP-2026-0144
Claimant
Stefan Müller
Studio Berlin · client
Respondent
Tomás Oliveira
cand-019 · 🇵🇹 Lisbon
Disputed amount

claim: 60% milestone refund

milestone 2 · paused

Daniel K.

Daniel has 1 business day to lock his decision before this auto-escalates to admin (you). Decision drafted Apr 30 · awaiting both parties' acknowledgement of proposed split before lock.

ADMIN ACTIONS
01 · 06

Dispute submission

Filed by claimant · 6 days 14 hours ago
CLAIMANT Stefan Müller's claim
Filed Apr 24, 2026 14:08 CET · via in-thread Dispute control
SM
Stefan Müller
Founder, Studio Berlin (Atelier Werkraum GmbH) · cl-002

Reason category: Work Quality · most common after Hours Tracking

The DevOps deliverable for Milestone 2 does not meet the specification we agreed in the contract. Tomás was contracted to set up a Kubernetes cluster on AWS with proper IAM scoping, monitoring (Prometheus + Grafana), and CI/CD pipelines for our 4 services. What we received is a working cluster, but:

  • IAM uses a single broad role for all services rather than per-service scoping (security concern)
  • Grafana dashboards are generic Prometheus templates · no service-specific metrics
  • CI/CD only covers 2 of 4 services · Tomás said the remaining 2 were "out of scope" (they were not)

Per the milestone agreement, milestone 2 acceptance criteria explicitly includes "service-scoped IAM" and "service-specific dashboards." I am requesting 60% refund of milestone 2 ($2,550) for the gap between agreed scope and delivered work. I'm willing to pay the remaining 40% ($1,700) for the work that was completed correctly.

"Atlas time-tracking data shows Tomás logged 47 hours on this milestone. The CI/CD half-build alone explains maybe 15-20 of those hours. The remaining IAM and dashboard gaps are not partial-credit issues — they're scope items he chose not to do." — Stefan Müller, dispute submission, Apr 24 14:08 CET
REQUESTED REFUND $2,550 · 60% of milestone 2 escrow
EVIDENCE SUBMITTED · 5 FILES
Milestone-2-spec.pdf
PDF · 142 KB · signed Apr 1
REVIEWED ✓
aws-iam-screenshot.png
PNG · 1.2 MB · captured Apr 23
REVIEWED ✓
grafana-dashboards.png
PNG · 890 KB · captured Apr 23
REVIEWED ✓
slack-thread-export.pdf
PDF · 312 KB · 38 messages
REVIEWED ✓
github-pr-comments.pdf
PDF · 78 KB · 14 comments
REVIEWED ✓
02 · 06

Respondent's response

Filed Apr 25, 2026 · 22h after claim
RESPONDENT Tomás Oliveira's response
Filed Apr 25, 2026 11:42 WET · within Atlas-recommended 24h window
TO
Tomás Oliveira
DevOps Engineer · cand-019 · Lisbon, Portugal

I disagree with Stefan's interpretation of the milestone scope, and the requested 60% refund is excessive. I believe the work delivered substantially meets the agreed milestone, with one legitimate gap that I'm willing to address.

On IAM scoping: The original spec said "appropriate IAM for the cluster," not "per-service IAM." Per-service IAM scoping is a security best practice but requires significant additional work (estimated 12–15h) that wasn't budgeted. I raised this in our Slack on Apr 12 and Stefan agreed to defer it. I have screenshots of that conversation.

On Grafana dashboards: I delivered the four standard Prometheus dashboards (Node, Pods, Deployments, Ingress). The spec said "Grafana setup with monitoring" — service-specific dashboards were never explicitly listed as deliverables. I'm happy to build them but they were not in scope.

On CI/CD coverage: This is the legitimate gap. I covered 2 of 4 services. The spec says "CI/CD for the platform services" without listing all four. I read it as the two main services. I'm willing to credit back $850 (50% of pro-rated time on those 2 services).

"I want to resolve this fairly. I'm not contesting that the CI/CD coverage is incomplete by Stefan's reading. But the IAM and dashboard items were not in the agreed scope, and 60% is asking me to give back work that was actually done correctly. A $850 credit covers the ambiguous portion." — Tomás Oliveira, response, Apr 25 11:42 WET
COUNTER-OFFER $850 · 20% of milestone 2 (CI/CD pro-rata)
RESPONDENT EVIDENCE · 4 FILES
slack-iam-defer-Apr12.png
PNG · 642 KB · 4 messages
REVIEWED ✓
milestone-2-spec-annotated.pdf
PDF · 168 KB · my reading
REVIEWED ✓
time-log-export.csv
CSV · 22 KB · 47h logged
REVIEWED ✓
git-history-summary.pdf
PDF · 94 KB · 38 commits
REVIEWED ✓
03 · 06

Specialist's investigation

Daniel Kovács · 6 days investigating · 14 actions logged
Daniel's investigation workspace
Editable until decision locked · admin can read · candidate + client cannot see
DANIEL'S NOTES ON THE CASE
Apr 30, 2026 · 16:18 CET — Updated. Both parties reviewed at length. After re-reading the milestone-2 spec four times, the truth is genuinely in the middle: On IAM: The spec says "appropriate IAM for the cluster" which is ambiguous. Tomás did raise the per-service-vs-shared distinction in Slack Apr 12, and Stefan did say "let's defer that" — but the deferral was specifically for "per-service IAM with rotation policies" not the broader scoping. I think Tomás reasonably interpreted that as deferring all per-service work, and Stefan reasonably remembers it as deferring only the rotation piece. Genuinely ambiguous. On Grafana dashboards: The spec says "Grafana setup with monitoring." That's ambiguous as written. Industry standard for "monitoring" on a 4-service platform would include service-specific dashboards. But it's not unreasonable to read "monitoring" as just "the four core Prometheus dashboards." Tomás's reading is defensible but on the thin side. On CI/CD: This one is clearer. The spec is genuinely silent on coverage scope. Tomás picking 2 of 4 was a unilateral decision, even if the spec didn't explicitly require all 4. Tomás owes more than $850 here. Recommendation: Split the difference. $1,700 refund to Stefan (40% of milestone 2). $2,550 released to Tomás. This treats CI/CD as Tomás's gap (worth ~$1,200), and the IAM + dashboard ambiguity as a 50/50 split (worth ~$500 to client side).
EVIDENCE REVIEWED · 9 ITEMS
Milestone-2 spec PDF · re-read 3× Apr 25 18:14
AWS IAM screenshots vs. industry baseline Apr 26 10:42
Grafana dashboards — quality assessed Apr 26 14:18

Time-tracker logs · 47h breakdown Apr 27 14:42
Git history · 38 commits · file scope Apr 28 11:14
GitHub PR comments · stakeholder review Apr 28 16:48
Slack thread export · 38-msg history Apr 29 10:08
External AWS DevOps consultant 2nd opinion declined · cost
CONVERSATIONS WITH PARTIES
Daniel held 3 calls with each party over the last 6 days · 1× joint mediation call (Apr 28) · 14 messages exchanged in admin-only thread · all calls recorded with consent and stored under DSP-2026-0144 audit trail.
Key conversation excerpts
3 of 142 messages · selected by Daniel as decisional
SM
Stefan Müller Apr 12, 2026 · 14:42 CET FLAGGED · KEY
"Yeah let's defer the per-service IAM with rotation for now — that's a lot of work and we can come back to it. Get the cluster running first."
TO
Tomás Oliveira Apr 12, 2026 · 14:48 CET FLAGGED · KEY
"OK 👍 will use a single IAM role for the cluster for now. Let me know when you want to revisit per-service."
SM
Stefan Müller Apr 18, 2026 · 09:14 CET CI/CD
"Where are the pipelines for the other two services? I assumed all four. Can you also do those before we mark m2 complete?"
04 · 06

Specialist's proposed decision

Drafted Apr 30 · awaiting both-party acknowledgement
Proposed split: 40% client / 60% candidate
DRAFT · NOT LOCKED
CLIENT REFUND
Stefan Müller (Studio Berlin)
−$1,700
40% of milestone 2 escrow refunded · less than the $2,550 requested
CANDIDATE PAYOUT
Tomás Oliveira
+$2,550
60% of milestone 2 escrow released · more than the $850 counter-offer
DANIEL'S RATIONALE
CI/CD coverage is the one clear gap. Tomás chose to cover 2 of 4 services unilaterally, and the spec is silent on scope. That gap is worth approximately $1,200 of the disputed amount.

IAM scoping and Grafana dashboards are genuinely ambiguous. The Apr 12 Slack exchange shows Stefan deferring "per-service IAM with rotation" — a narrower deferral than Tomás interpreted, but a real one. Splitting the remaining $1,000 of disputed amount 50/50 reflects that ambiguity fairly.

Net result: $1,700 refund to Stefan ($1,200 + $500), $2,550 released to Tomás. Both parties get a result they can live with — neither got what they asked for, but neither lost decisively. Decision does not require either party to redo work.
Status: Stefan acknowledged Apr 30 17:42 CET. Tomás's ack pending — last seen 4h ago. Auto-locks at SLA breach (May 1, 14:08 CET) if not contested.

ROUTING
Apr 24
Dispute opened · Stefan Müller (claimant)
Filed via in-thread Dispute control · 5 evidence files · escrow milestone-2 auto-paused at $4,250 · DSP-2026-0144 created
CREATED
REASONS FOR PENDING ESCALATION
If Tomás contests the proposed decision (he has 36h to do so), the case auto-escalates to Stage 4 (Admin decision) — which lands on your desk. Tomás's $850 counter is significantly below Daniel's $2,550 candidate-side proposal, but Daniel's split arguably gives Tomás more than he asked for ($2,550 vs his $1,700 = $850 counter-implied). Likelihood of escalation: low (≤25%). Stefan has already acknowledged.

Fraud & abuse › Critical › FA-2026-0042 · Vorona Capital + 4 linked
FA-2026-0042 Critical Investigating Detected May 2 · 18:42 UTC · 3d 14h ago

Multi-account fraud ring · Vorona Capital + 4 linked accounts

Lead account Vorona Capital (cl-167) shares device fingerprint, IP block, payment instrument, and behavioral patterns with 4 other client accounts. Cross-references prior signals from Disputes (5 disputes · 42% rate) and Reviews (suspected coordinated review activity).
Investigation
External
Confidence
94%
8 of 9 signals fired
Linked accounts
5
1 lead · 4 satellite
Financial impact
$28.4K
across 12 disputed engagements
Investigation age
3d 14h
SLA: 7 days for critical
01

System signals that triggered the alert

9 signals evaluated · 8 fired · last evaluated 3 min ago
1
Shared device fingerprint
All 5 accounts log in from the same browser fingerprint hash (Canvas + WebGL + UA + fonts) · cross-account match within 3-day window
98%
2
Shared IP block
All 5 accounts originate from /24 IP block 217.182.143.0 (DigitalOcean datacenter, Frankfurt) · no residential / mobile diversification
96%
3
Shared payment instrument
Stripe payment method pm_4Xb…91N appears as default payment for 4 of 5 accounts
99%
4
Behavioral pattern · session timing
All accounts active in same 4-hour window daily (06:00–10:00 UTC) · synchronized job-posting rhythm
88%
5
Dispute clustering
42% dispute rate on Vorona Capital + 28-37% on satellite accounts · all hours-tracking + payment-delay disputes
94%
6
KYB document anomaly
2 of 5 accounts submitted KYB documents with same company-registration scan (different filed names) · OCR similarity 94%
91%
7
Coordinated review activity
Cross-references Step 13 cluster — 11 reviews from 5 reviewer accounts · all 5-star · all within 9 days · same writing fingerprint
87%
8
Geo / declared-location mismatch
Declared client locations (Berlin, Madrid, Lisbon, Vienna, Bratislava) inconsistent with actual login geo (all Frankfurt DC)
82%
9
VPN / proxy / Tor detection
No VPN, proxy, or Tor traffic detected · accounts use direct datacenter routing · this is unusual but not in itself a fraud indicator
—

03

Behavioral anomalies

deviation from normal client behavior · last 30 days
Synchronized session timing
All 5 accounts active 06:00–10:00 UTC daily · normal clients diversify across timezones · σ = 0.4h vs platform avg 4.2h
+10.5σ
Job-posting velocity
5.2 jobs/account/week · platform median is 0.4 · all jobs in same 6 categories (engineering, design, content)
+13σ
Hire decision speed
Avg 2.1 hours from posting to hire · platform avg is 8.2 days · suggests pre-arranged talent
+5.8σ
Payment timing
All 5 accounts pay invoices on day 28-29 of payment terms · creates 3-4d delay disputes pattern
+3.2σ
Review-leaving cadence
All 5 accounts leave reviews within 18-26h of engagement close · 100% are 5-star · normal distribution is 2.4d-7d
+8.4σ

05

Timeline of events

all relevant events from Jan 18 (account creation) → today · 142 audit entries
Today · 10:18 UTC · 38 min ago
Aïsha Okafor opened decision review
Reviewing all signals + cross-references before final action. Considering: Ban all 5 accounts + refund affected talent.
Today · 09:42 UTC
Sarah Reyes added admin note
"Talent payouts of $11.4K already disbursed across 14 affected candidates — those are protected. New refund needs to come from Atlas reserves, not clawback."
May 5 · 08:45 UTC
System linked dispute + review evidence
Cross-referenced DSP-2026-0144 (Stefan/Tomás dispute) and REV-834 (sock-puppet review cluster) to this fraud alert. All three cases now reference each other.
May 4 · 11:08 UTC
Dario Fonseca compiled evidence pack
23 documents · 14-day log range · cross-account fingerprint correlation matrix. Ready for legal review if escalation needed.
May 3 · 09:22 UTC
System identified linked accounts
4 satellite accounts flagged: cl-201 (Eastline), cl-219 (Pintar), cl-228 (Avant), cl-235 (Branka). All 4 added to active watchlist with auto-suspend trigger if any takes a high-value action.
May 2 · 19:14 UTC
Aïsha Okafor began initial triage
Confirmed alert is not a false positive. Signal accuracy verified manually for top 5 signals. Investigation opened formally.
May 2 · 18:42 UTC
System raised CRITICAL alert
8 of 9 fraud signals fired simultaneously on cl-167 (Vorona Capital). Alert FA-2026-0042 created. Notification sent to fraud-alerts Slack channel + paged Aïsha (lead admin).
Apr 30 · 11:42 UTC
Earlier flag — sock-puppet reviews (medium)
FA-2026-0038 raised on A2Z Solutions (cl-178) for review cluster. Same review-writing fingerprint detected — investigation revealed Vorona ring as upstream source.
Mar 14 · earlier
Vorona dispute pattern noted
Vorona's 5th dispute in 90d triggered "high dispute rate" client flag (Step 12 patterns). At the time, no fraud ring detected — only billing pattern.
Jan 18 · earlier
Vorona Capital account created
cl-167 onboarded · KYB passed · authorized signatory verified · trust tier "Standard" assigned.

DF
Dario Fonseca Super Admin May 4 · 11:14 UTC
Evidence pack done. The KYB document OCR similarity is the strongest piece — same scan reused across 2 different submissions with different filed names. That alone is enough for ban + legal-report. Aïsha, you have my pre-authorization for the ban action when you're ready.
SR
Sarah Reyes Specialist May 4 · 09:30 UTC
All 14 affected talents have been paid out per their contracts (verified by Finance). Their payouts are honored regardless of what we do to Vorona — talent is not at risk financially. Affected list attached as doc-007.
AO
Aïsha Okafor (you) Operations May 3 · 14:22 UTC
Pinged @dario for super-authorization on hard ban. Reasoning: 8/9 signals fired, KYB document fraud is criminal-grade, dispute pattern + sock-puppet reviews show this is sustained abuse of platform. Recommend ban + 30-day hold on funds + regulatory notification to BaFin (Frankfurt jurisdiction).
AO
Add note as Aïsha You

Security incidents › Critical › SI-2026-0014 · Mongo backup S3 exposure
SI-2026-0014 Critical In investigation Data breach Detected May 4 · 14:18 UTC · 1d 18h ago

Mongo backup snapshot exposed via misconfigured S3 bucket

A Mongo backup snapshot containing the candidates table was found accessible via an S3 bucket misconfigured with public-list permissions. Exposed: 8,432 records (names, emails, phone, country, signup-date). Excluded: passwords, payment data, ID documents, session tokens. Bucket secured at 14:34 UTC; 3-hour exposure window. Cross-reference fraud alert FA-2026-0042 not triggered (different vector).
Coordination
Lifecycle
Affected users

candidates · partial PII
Exposure window
3h 16m
11:18 → 14:34 UTC May 4
Detection method
External
researcher disclosure to security@

53h 22m
36% of 72h budget remaining
GDPR ARTICLE 33 · BREACH NOTIFICATION CLOCK
Time to notify supervisory authority
Clock active
02
Days left
05

14
Seconds
26h 22m elapsed · 53h 22m remaining of 72h GDPR window Draft notification draft →
01

Notification status

affected users · regulatory authorities · all notifications audit-logged
Affected users
Partial · in-flight
Partial
Notified
5,398 / 8,432
In-progress
3,034
Channels

142 · retry

Regulatory authorities

Required
GDPR · supervisory
Drafted · not filed
CCPA · CPPA
Pending review
LGPD (BR)
Not triggered
Owner
Dario · Compliance
Deadline: 53h 22m remaining (GDPR)
02

Incident description

root-cause analysis in progress · technical narrative below

At May 4 · 11:18 UTC, an automated database backup process wrote a Mongo snapshot of the candidates collection to S3 bucket atlas-prod-backups-archive. A Terraform change merged earlier the same day inadvertently set block_public_acls = false on this bucket, allowing the snapshot's default ACL to expose it to anonymous list operations.

At 14:18 UTC, an external security researcher (bug-bounty submission B-2104) reported the bucket via security@atlas.com. Containment by IAM-policy revert + bucket-policy-deny was completed at 14:34 UTC, closing a 3 hour 16 minute exposure window.

Exposed records: 8,432 candidate accounts · fields: name, email, phone, country, signup-date, profile-headline. Excluded: passwords (separately stored), payment instruments (Stripe-tokenized only), uploaded ID documents (separate encrypted store), session tokens.

CloudTrail logs confirm 2 anonymous list operations against the bucket during the window — both from the researcher's IP. No download / GetObject events recorded. Exposure is limited to record enumeration; the researcher confirmed no record contents were accessed beyond verifying breach feasibility. We are treating it as a confirmed Article 33-eligible event regardless, given the discoverability window.

03

Actions taken

7 actions logged · 3 completed · 1 in progress · 3 pending · all audit-stamped
1
May 4 · 14:34
Revoked public-list ACL on atlas-prod-backups-archive
Reverted Terraform diff. Applied bucket-policy deny statement. Verified inaccessibility from anonymous client. Containment confirmed at 14:34:08 UTC.
RJ by Rohan Joshi (SRE on-call)
2
May 4 · 15:02
Forensic snapshot captured · CloudTrail + bucket-access logs preserved
14-day window of CloudTrail events archived to forensic store · MD5 hashed · chain-of-custody log started. 2 anonymous list operations confirmed from researcher IP only.
LC by Lin Chen (AppSec lead)
3
May 4 · 16:48
Affected-user list compiled · 8,432 records · cohort analysis
Generated reproducible affected-user query from snapshot diff. By region: 4,184 EU · 1,927 US · 2,321 ROW. Notification template approved by Erin (Legal) and queued for staged send.
AO by Aïsha Okafor (you)
4
May 5 · 09:14
Affected-user notification rollout · staged send
Email + WhatsApp (where opted-in) · 1,000 recipients per 30-min batch to avoid bounce / abuse heuristics. 5,398 of 8,432 sent. ETA complete: today 18:00 UTC.
SY by System · Aïsha oversight
5
Pending · 17:00
File GDPR Article 33 supervisory-authority notice
Draft prepared by Dario · awaiting Legal sign-off (Erin) · target submission via supervisory portal before 17:40 UTC today. Includes affected-user count, data classes, mitigation, intended remediation.
DF assigned to Dario Fonseca
6
Pending · May 6
Eradication · IAM-policy hardening + Terraform CI guard
Add tflint rule blocking block_public_acls = false on backup buckets. Enforce SCP at org-level. Quarterly review cadence.
LC assigned to Lin Chen
7
Pending · May 18
Post-mortem published · cross-team review
14-day post-incident post-mortem with timeline, contributing factors, lessons, action-items. Distributed to all-hands. Section pending until incident closes.
AO assigned to Aïsha Okafor
04

Timeline of events

all UTC · 86 audit-log entries · 24h+ since detection
Today · 09:14 UTC · 1h 12m ago
Aïsha Okafor began notification rollout
Staged batch send started. 5,398 of 8,432 affected users notified by email + WhatsApp where opted in. Bounce-rate normal (1.7%) · 142 retries queued.
Today · 08:30 UTC
Dario Fonseca drafted GDPR Article 33 notice
Initial draft prepared per supervisory authority template. Forwarded to Erin (Legal) for sign-off. Target file: today 17:40 UTC.
May 4 · 16:48 UTC
Aïsha Okafor compiled affected-user cohort
8,432 records identified via snapshot diff against current Mongo. Notification email + WA template drafted, approved by Legal, queued for staged send.
May 4 · 15:02 UTC
Lin Chen captured forensic snapshot
CloudTrail + S3 access logs archived. Anonymous list events traced — only 2 events, both from researcher's reported IP. No GetObject / DownloadFile events. Chain-of-custody log started.
May 4 · 14:34 UTC
Rohan Joshi contained bucket exposure
Reverted Terraform diff + applied explicit bucket-policy deny. Containment verified by anonymous-client probe. Exposure window closed at 3h 16m.
May 4 · 14:31 UTC
System auto-escalated to CRITICAL · paged on-call
Affected-user count exceeded 1,000 threshold. Severity bumped CRITICAL. PagerDuty paged Aïsha (incident lead), Dario (compliance), Rohan (SRE on-call). War-room created: #sec-si-0014.
May 4 · 14:21 UTC
Aïsha Okafor opened incident SI-2026-0014
Triaged researcher report, confirmed plausibility within 3 minutes via direct probe of bucket. Severity initial: HIGH. Status: NEW.
May 4 · 14:18 UTC
External bug-bounty disclosure received
Researcher submission to security@atlas.com · attached bucket URL + 5 sample object listings. Auto-routed to security alias inbox. Acknowledgement sent within 4 minutes.
May 4 · 11:18 UTC
Backup snapshot written to S3 (event horizon)
Automated nightly backup job executed at 11:18 UTC. Snapshot inherited bucket ACL. Bucket made publicly listable by Terraform change merged at 09:42 UTC same day.
May 4 · 09:42 UTC
Root cause introduced — Terraform PR #2418 merged
PR set block_public_acls = false on archive bucket as part of an unrelated cross-account-replication change. Reviewers missed the line. Lint-rule for this is the Section 05 remediation.
05

Post-mortem

due May 18 · 14d after detection · published when complete
Pending · due May 18
Post-mortem will be drafted after the incident closes.
Expected sections include: timeline, root cause, contributing factors, mitigation, lessons learned, follow-up action items, regulatory outcomes, and a public summary.

Owner: Aïsha Okafor · review by Dario + Lin · all-hands distribution.
Reference · how the SI-2026-0007 (Auth0) post-mortem looked

SI-2026-0007 · Auth0 vendor advisory · post-mortem

Published · Apr 26
Root cause
Upstream vendor security advisory (CVE-2026-0142). No Atlas tenant impact confirmed. Forced session refresh issued as a precaution.
Mitigation
Forced re-authentication for 28K users · session-token rotation · monitoring enhanced for 14 days post-event.
Lessons learned
Vendor-advisory triage process now formalized into runbook. Time-to-precautionary-action: 4h 12m (target ≤ 6h).
Action items
3 / 3 completed · vendor-advisory dashboard · session-rotation playbook · quarterly tabletop exercise scheduled.

6

SB-2026-0083 Suspended · 7 days Fake ID / liveness Imposed May 4 · 09:31 UTC · 1d 23h ago

Marek Kowalczyk · 7-day suspension · Identity-document fraud

Account cand-1142 suspended for failing the FA-2026-0041 identity check (94% confidence). Submitted ID document does not match liveness photo · separate facial-geometry analysis flags 91% likelihood of impersonation. Appeal filed by user May 5 · currently under admin review.

MK
Marek Kowalczyk May 5 · 14:08 UTC
"The face-match flag is a false positive. The ID I uploaded is genuine — it was issued in 2019 after I had significant weight loss and a beard. The current liveness selfie shows me 6 years later, clean-shaven, with weight regained after a long illness. I have additional supporting documents (medical records, second photo ID with current appearance, social media history) that I would like to submit. I respectfully ask for a manual review with the additional evidence. I have been an active candidate for 4 months with positive engagement history."
Pending your decision. Review the user's statement + 3 attachments + original FA-2026-0041 evidence. Then approve (lift suspension) or deny (suspension stands). Both actions notify the user automatically.
02

Reason & audit trail

why this sanction was imposed · chain of decisions · all entries audit-logged
Primary reason
Identity-document fraud — uploaded ID does not match liveness selfie
Trust & Safety policy section 4.2(a). Severity: HIGH. Standard sanction tier: 7-day suspension with appeal route (1st offense) → ban (2nd offense). Source: FA-2026-0041 · facial-geometry mismatch · 91% confidence.
May 5 · 14:09 UTC Appeal eligibility check passed (auto)
User filed within 30-day appeal window. Account in good standing apart from this sanction. Routed to admin review queue.
May 5 · 14:08 UTC Appeal filed by user via in-app appeal form
Marek submitted statement + 3 attachments. SLA clock started · 48h to decision · auto-escalates if missed.
May 4 · 09:32 UTC User notified of suspension auto · email + WA
Standard suspension notice sent in EN + PL (user's profile language). Includes reason summary, expiration date, appeal-route link.
May 4 · 09:31 UTC 7-day suspension imposed by Aïsha Okafor
After review of FA-2026-0041 (94% confidence). Sanction tier picked per policy 4.2(a): suspension with appeal route, not direct ban. Reason fields completed; supporting evidence linked.
May 4 · 09:18 UTC Fraud alert raised system · auto-escalation
FA-2026-0041 created at CRITICAL severity. Auto-paged Aïsha. Account auto-restricted from new applications pending review (precaution; not a formal sanction yet).
Jan 12 · earlier Account created
cand-1142 onboarded · KYC initial pass · 4 months active history · 2 successful engagements · 4.6 ★ avg rating · no prior sanctions.

04

Communication with user

direct messages between admin and Marek · separate from internal admin notes · user has read receipts
MK
Hi, I just received the suspension email. I'm confused — the ID I uploaded is genuine. The face match issue is because I look very different now after a long illness. Is there a way to provide additional verification?
May 4 · 11:42 UTC · received via WA
Hi Marek — yes, you can file an appeal through the in-app appeal form. You'll have the chance to attach supporting documents (a current ID, medical records, anything that helps establish identity continuity). The appeal will be reviewed within 48 hours of filing. The appeal link is in the suspension email and also visible on your sign-in page.
Aïsha · May 4 · 12:05 UTC
AO
MK
Thank you, I'll prepare the documents and file the appeal today.
May 4 · 13:22 UTC · received via WA
MK
Appeal filed with 3 attachments. Please let me know if you need anything else from my side. Thank you for the clear process.
May 5 · 14:11 UTC · received via in-app
Send via: Email + WhatsApp · Marek has both opted in

visible to admins only · not visible to user · all timestamps UTC
DF
Dario Fonseca Super Admin May 5 · 16:30 UTC
Reviewed the appeal. The supporting evidence is convincing — medical records show documented weight change and beard removal during the period. Facial-geometry mismatch is consistent with significant physical change. I'd lean toward approving the appeal but want Aïsha to confirm. Aïsha — your call.
SR
Sarah Reyes Specialist May 5 · 15:14 UTC
Worked with Marek twice last quarter — both engagements went well. Communication style and writing match the appeal voice. No prior flags. Adding context for the review.

Suspicious activity › New › SA-2026-1042 · Rohan Kapoor · geo anomaly
SA-2026-1042 New · 14s ago Geo anomaly Detected 10:24:46 UTC · auto-flagged · unreviewed

4 logins from 3 countries in 18 minutes — possible session hijacking

Rohan Kapoor (cand-1289) logged in from 4 distinct IPs across India → Germany → France → Germany within 18 minutes. Each transition exceeds plausible physical travel time. AI confidence: 71% suspicious. Could be: (a) credential compromise, (b) coordinated VPN test, or (c) a legitimate user with multiple devices on different networks. Account flagged for review · no auto-action taken.
Signal strength
4 / 5
High · auto-pages on 5
AI confidence
71%
suspicious vs benign
Transitions
3 countries
in 18 min · ~4500km traversal
Pattern siblings
6 events
from 6 accounts · same hour
PATTERN DETECTED · CLUSTER 2026-04-A
Possibly part of a wider "rapid geo-traversal" cluster
Sibling events
6
from 6 different accounts in last 60 min
Common IP block
/16
3 of 6 share the Frankfurt DC range
Cluster confidence
84%
if confirmed, escalates to FA-2026-XXXX
Six accounts — all candidates — have shown rapid geo-traversal patterns within the last hour. Three (50%) share a common IP block in Frankfurt that overlaps with the Vorona Capital ring (FA-2026-0042). This may be coincidence (datacenter VPN, common ISP) or coordination. Recommended: watchlist all 6 for 48h and re-cluster after the next round of events. Auto-watchlist already applied to siblings; this event is the most recent.

Invoice INV-2026-1142 for Daniel Kovács's design engagement (ENG-1142) with Studio Berlin (cl-002). Stripe declined the charge with code card_declined · insufficient_funds. May 6 · 10:14:08 UTC ±€0.00
Charge declined by network · payment_intent failed
Stripe returned card_declined with reason insufficient_funds. Network response code 51. No funds moved. Auto-retry queued for May 7 · 10:14 UTC (24h spacing per retry policy).
May 6 · 10:14:02 UTC —
Charge attempt sent to Stripe
payment_intent pi_3O2K8m…91N confirmed via API. 3DS not required (card previously verified Mar 14). Attempt 1 of 3.

INV-2026-1142 reached payment-due-date · auto-charge cron picked up the invoice · charge instruction created.
May 5 · 09:00 UTC —

Engagement ENG-1142 milestone "v2 design system delivered" verified by client (Studio Berlin). Invoice generated · €2,840 EUR · payment due May 6.

Studio Berlin signed contract for ENG-1142. Auto-charge enabled. Payment method •••• 4242 selected.

Fees & pricing

/admin/finance/fees-pricing · last fee change Apr 18 · 4 active promo codes · audit log 23 events
· · · · · ·
All fee modifications require Super Admin role + password re-entry on confirm. You (Aïsha Okafor) have Operations Admin — view-only access. Dario Fonseca can make changes. Promo-code management is delegated and available to Operations.

Current fee structure

in effect since Apr 18 2026 · applied to all engagements and re-certifications
v2.4 · 23 historical changes
PRIMARY · CLIENT
Client fee on hire
10% of contract value
Applied at the moment a client confirms a hire and the engagement contract is signed. Net of Stripe processing, before Wise FX. This is Atlas's primary revenue line — $138.7K of $138.7K fee revenue in last 90d.
In effect since Apr 18 2026 · prior rate was 8% (founder pricing) · view change history →
PRIMARY · CANDIDATE
Candidate fee on hire
0% · free for talent
Talent keeps the full negotiated rate, minus Wise FX (pass-through). This is an intentional positioning choice — Atlas is free for talent, paid by employers. Reviewed at every quarterly board meeting.
Stable since launch · view change history →

Re-certification fees

paid by candidates · charged only on re-takes after a failed attempt · first attempt always free
Test
Price
30d volume
English assessment · re-take
written + listening · auto-graded · 60 min
$20USD
142$2,840 revenue · 30d
Interview 1 · re-take
generalist · 30 min with Specialist
$30USD
68$2,040 revenue · 30d
Interview 2 · per category · re-take
category specialist · 45 min · technical assessment
$50USD
42$2,100 revenue · 30d

Pass-through fees

Atlas does not mark these up · they apply to all transactions and are paid by the party initiating
S
Stripe payment processing
Standard EU rates apply · 1.4% + €0.25 for EU cards · 2.9% + $0.30 for international · paid by the client
W
Wise currency conversion + payout
Mid-market FX rate + Wise's standard fee (≈ 0.6%) · paid by the talent (deducted from payout)

all changes require Super Admin password re-entry · 7-day announcement notice to users · audit logged

Pricing tiers

Premium and Enterprise tiers are roadmapped for Q3 · current platform runs single-tier · the cards below are configuration previews
Standard
10% per hire
  • Unlimited hires
  • All 22 talent categories
  • Specialist support · 48h SLA
  • Standard payment terms · Net 30
Premium
$499/ mo + 8% per hire
  • Everything in Standard
  • Lower per-hire fee (8%)
  • Priority Specialist support · 12h SLA
  • Custom payment terms · Net 60
  • Talent shortlist within 24h
Enterprise
Custom· contact sales
  • Everything in Premium
  • Negotiated per-hire fee
  • Dedicated Account Manager
  • SSO + SCIM + custom reporting
  • SLA-backed contract

Launch promo LAUNCH50 auto-expired · by System (scheduled)
"Scheduled expiry per original promo setup. 204 uses · $61.2K total discount granted during launch month. Successor code SPRING2026 created same day."
Jan 14 2026 · 16:30 UTC FC-2026-015
Initial fee structure set · by Dario Fonseca (Super Admin · founder)
Client fee: 8% · Candidate fee: 0% · Re-cert: $20 / $30 / $40
"Platform launch pricing. 8% founder rate, intentionally lower than the 10% target rate, for first 90 days. Re-certification fees set per cost analysis of Specialist time."

Refund-pattern insights · last 90 days

  • Studio Berlin (cl-002) is the only client with refund rate over 5% · 4 refunds, all dispute-resolution · same engagement category (design) · review client →
  • Dispute resolution is the dominant reason at 38% of refunds and 44% of refund volume · suggests value in pre-engagement clarity (clearer deliverable specs, milestone definitions)
  • Vorona ring (cl-167 + 4 satellites) accounts for 3 of the last 90-day refunds · all flagged at issue · part of the wider FA-2026-0042 case · banned
  • Engineering category has 2× the refund rate of other categories (1.2% vs 0.6% avg) · primarily from quality-issue refunds · investigate Specialist screening

Legal requests › Subpoenas › LR-2026-0023 · SDNY · Vorona Capital
LR-2026-0023 Under review Grand Jury Subpoena US District Court · SDNY Received May 4 · 7d ago · case 1:26-cr-00284-AKH

Grand jury subpoena re: Vorona Capital + 4 linked entities — criminal investigation

Issued by AUSA Sarah Chen, US Attorney's Office, Southern District of New York. Seeks platform records (KYC, transactions, communications, login records) for the Vorona ring identified in FA-2026-0042 (Step 15) and banned in SB-2026-0084. Response coordinated through outside counsel Cooley LLP (Michael Reeves). Atlas is not a target — production-only request.
Document
Procedural
Archive
Deadline
May 25
14d remaining · 50% elapsed
Issuing AUSA
Sarah Chen
SDNY · criminal division
Atlas counsel
Cooley LLP
Michael Reeves · partner
Records sought
5 categories
scope locked · all sub-§ confirmed
Gag order in effect · 90 days
Per 18 U.S.C. § 2705(b) non-disclosure order issued with this subpoena. Do NOT notify Vorona Capital, its principals, or any of the 4 linked entities of the existence of this request, its contents, or any production activity. Violation may result in obstruction charges. Order expires Aug 2, 2026 unless extended.
83 days until lift
renewable
REQUIRED ACTION

Produce 5 categories of records by May 25, 2026 · responding counsel: Cooley LLP

What we must produce
Platform records
5 categories · scope detailed below · ~2,800 documents identified for review
By when
May 25 · 17:00 EST
14 days remaining · production to AUSA office via secured FTP
Responsible counsel
Cooley LLP
Michael Reeves · partner · government investigations · responding under our SOW
01

Original document

subpoena filed under seal · 12 pages · signed by AUSA Sarah Chen · received via SDNY Service Department
subpoena_sdny_1-26-cr-00284-akh_vorona-capital.pdf
12 pages · 4.2 MB · signed + sealed · received May 4 · 11:14 EST · chain of custody preserved
United
States
Courts
Seal
United States District Court
for the Southern District of New York
UNITED STATES OF AMERICA
       v.
SEALED
Case No. 1:26-cr-00284-AKH
Grand Jury Subpoena

TO: Staffva LLC, a Michigan limited liability company, doing business as "Atlas". Service via registered agent, Detroit, MI.

YOU ARE COMMANDED to produce the documents, electronically stored information, and objects specified in Attachment A by May 25, 2026 at 5:00 P.M. Eastern Time. Production shall be made to the Office of the United States Attorney for the Southern District of New York via secured FTP per Attachment B, or in such other manner as may be arranged with the undersigned.

The matters under investigation involve potential violations of 18 U.S.C. §§ 1343 (wire fraud), 1956 (money laundering), and 371 (conspiracy). The Grand Jury seeks records concerning the platform accounts and activity of certain entities listed in Attachment A, including "Vorona Capital" and affiliated entities.

02

Subject & record scope

5 entities named · 5 record categories · scope locked at intake · counsel-confirmed

all banned May 5 · linked through Vorona ring · audit trail in FA-2026-0042

entities

Collected

all sign-in events · pending engineering pull · ETA May 13

Pending
03

Response draft

3 versions · counsel-drafted · v3 current · all versions preserved for legal record
Draft v3 · Cooley LLP final response
Michael Reeves · saved May 9 · 16:42 EST · awaiting Dario sign-off · ready for Atlas review
Pending review

Counsel for Staffva LLC d/b/a Atlas ("Producer") hereby submits this response to the Grand Jury Subpoena dated May 1, 2026 in the matter of United States v. Sealed, Case No. 1:26-cr-00284-AKH ("Subpoena").

Producer has identified and is producing the following categories of records responsive to Attachments A and B:

Bates VOR-000001 to VOR-000142. Account creation and KYC records for the five entities named in Attachment A § 1, including business registration documents, beneficial-ownership disclosures, government-issued IDs for principals, and identity verification artifacts. Produced as native PDF with embedded metadata. Bates VOR-000143 to VOR-001990. Transaction records for all five entities from account inception through May 5, 2026 (date of platform termination). Produced as a structured CSV with field-level documentation in accompanying README. Includes Stripe payment intent IDs, Wise transaction references, and Atlas internal IDs for cross-referencing. Bates VOR-001991 to VOR-002403. All recorded communications between Producer's personnel and the five entities, including in-app messaging, email correspondence, and dispute records. Personal information of Producer's employees has been redacted per FRCrP 6(e)(2) and redaction log filed as Attachment C.

Production timing. Categories 1-3 are ready for transmission upon Government's confirmation of receipt protocol. Categories 4-5 (login records and linked-account analysis) are in active collection and will be produced no later than the May 25, 2026 deadline.

Reservation. Producer reserves all rights, including but not limited to objections on the grounds of relevance, overbreadth, and any applicable privilege, and reserves the right to supplement or amend this production in writing.

Bates range planned: VOR-000001 to VOR-002800 · 5 categories · attachments A-C · production via SDNY secured FTP
04

Action timeline

chronological log of every action taken on this matter · timestamps in UTC · audit-locked
May 9 · 16:42 UTC Draft v3 saved by counsel by Michael Reeves (Cooley LLP)
Tightened Bates ranges, added reservation of rights, clarified production protocol for categories 4-5 still in collection. Sent to Atlas for sign-off.
May 7 · 11:08 UTC Draft v2 by counsel by Michael Reeves (Cooley LLP)
Restructured response to follow Atlas's preferred Bates conventions. Added FRCrP 6(e)(2) redaction language for employee PII.
May 6 · 14:22 UTC 3 of 5 record categories collected and exported to counsel by Dario Fonseca
KYC, transactions, communications exported via secured S3 bucket. Remaining 2 categories (login records + ring analysis) in active collection by Engineering + T&S teams.
May 5 · 09:15 UTC Draft v1 by Atlas legal team by Dario Fonseca
Initial response sketch with placeholder Bates ranges. Sent to Cooley for substantive drafting.
May 4 · 13:45 UTC Outside counsel engaged by Dario Fonseca
Cooley LLP engaged via standing SOW · Michael Reeves assigned as lead · privilege established · matter conflict-checked.
May 4 · 12:30 UTC Receipt acknowledged to SDNY by Dario Fonseca
Formal acknowledgment of receipt sent to AUSA Sarah Chen's office via certified email. Confirmed intent to respond by May 25 deadline. Confidentiality of gag order affirmed.
May 4 · 11:14 UTC Subpoena received via SDNY Service Department
Hand-delivered to Staffva LLC registered agent in Detroit, MI. Logged into compliance vault. Auto-routed to Dario (Super Admin) and Aïsha (Ops Admin) within 60s of receipt.
05

Internal legal notes

privileged work product · attorney-client communications · admin-only · do NOT export with response
MR
Michael Reeves Cooley LLP · outside counsel May 9 · 16:50 UTC
v3 is production-ready from our side. Reservation language follows the SDNY playbook we've used in similar matters. Recommend Atlas sign off by end of week — we want 5-7 business days buffer for transmission, FTP setup, and any government follow-up before May 25.
DF
Dario Fonseca Super Admin · matter lead May 7 · 18:30 UTC
Engineering team confirms login-record pull on track for May 13. T&S team confirms ring-analysis export will follow by May 14. Both well within our buffer for May 25. Will sign off on v3 once final read tomorrow.

Data subject rights › Deletion requests › DSR-2026-0089 · Marek Kowalczyk · Article 17
DSR-2026-0089 Processing Deletion · Art. 17 GDPR GDPR · Poland Filed Apr 28 · 13d ago · 30-day window

Article 17 deletion request from suspended candidate · competing legal obligations

Marek Kowalczyk (cand-1142) has invoked Article 17(1)(a) GDPR — "right to erasure" following his suspension in SB-2026-0083. Atlas asserts Article 17(3) exemptions: tax-retention obligations (Step 22 · IRS through 2032), fraud-defense interests (FA-2026-0042), legal-claims defense (open dispute risks), and breach-notification record-keeping (SI-2026-0014). DPO recommendation: partial deletion with retention markers.

01

Article 17 grounds analysis

subject's invoked right vs Atlas's overriding exemptions · DPO assessment · balancing test

Balancing test · Art. 17(1) vs Art. 17(3)

subject asserts 1 ground · Atlas asserts 4 exemptions · 3 strong, 1 weak
Pending DPO sign-off
Subject asserts
Right to erasure under GDPR Article 17
Art. 17(1)(a) Valid right
"Personal data are no longer necessary in relation to the purposes for which they were collected"
Subject argues: account suspended, no further engagements possible, therefore data is no longer necessary. Legally well-founded on its face. Atlas must establish overriding ground to refuse.
Subject also invokes
EDPB Guidelines 5/2020 on consent
Subject claims original consent was for active marketplace use only, and that suspension terminates the consent basis for further processing.
vs
Balancing
test
Atlas asserts (4 grounds)
Article 17(3) exemptions override the right to erasure
Art. 17(3)(b) Strong
Compliance with legal obligation (tax retention)
IRS §6501 requires retention of 1042-S filings + supporting records for 7 years. Marek's 2025 1042-S (Step 22 · $4,420 USD earnings) was filed Mar 14 — retention through Mar 2032. Cannot delete underlying data without violating US tax law.
Art. 17(3)(b) Strong
GDPR Article 30 records of processing
Atlas's own SI-2026-0014 breach record (Step 16) requires retaining identifiers of all data subjects affected. Marek's account was in the breach scope. Required for ICO follow-up (LR-2026-0017).
Art. 17(3)(e) Strong
Defense of legal claims (fraud)
Suspension SB-2026-0083 for fake-ID fraud is under appeal. Open FA-2026-0042 fraud-ring investigation references Marek's data for cluster analysis. Deletion would prejudice both Atlas's defense and the broader investigation.
Art. 17(3)(c) Weak
Public interest (broader fraud detection)
Atlas's anti-fraud signals improve when bad-actor data is retained for pattern matching. Weak ground: ICO has been skeptical of "platform safety" arguments without specific legal basis. Cite as supporting only.
DPO RECOMMENDATION
Partial deletion · retain 5 categories under Art. 17(3)(b) and (e)
Delete profile, marketing, communications, cookies, behavioral data (7 categories · approx 4,200 records). Retain financial records, fraud markers, audit logs, breach-scope identifiers, dispute records (5 categories · approx 280 records) with legal-basis tags. Notify Marek of partial fulfillment with specific Art. 17(3) citations. Document for Art. 30 record of processing. Estimated execution: 3-5 business days post-DPO sign-off.
02

Deletion scope preview

data categories with disposition · delete-side is irreversible · retain-side requires legal-basis tag · preview before execute

Scope · partial deletion · 12 categories analyzed

approx 4,480 records affected total · split per DPO recommendation

Will be deleted

7
Profile data 12 records
Name, photo, bio, headline, skills tags, location, languages, profile customizations · direct delete from primary store · backup purge in 90d
Communications history 2,847 records
In-app messages, support tickets, notification preferences · cascade delete via message-thread anonymization · counterparty data preserved
Marketing & preferences 28 records
Email subscriptions, push tokens, marketing consent records, A/B test cohort assignments · full purge from marketing platform (Customer.io)
Behavioral / analytics 1,180 records
Click events, page views, session recordings, search queries · full purge from analytics (Amplitude) + session-replay store
Cookie / device data 62 records
Cookie consent records, device fingerprints (non-fraud), session tokens · delete + revoke
Reviews authored 8 records
Reviews Marek left for clients · anonymize, not delete (clients have right to retain reviewed-feedback context) · author becomes "[removed user]"
Public profile mirror 1 record
Profile cache in CDN + SEO sitemap · invalidate + recrawl · 48h propagation

Will be retained · with legal basis

5
Financial records · tax 142 records
2025 earnings ($4,420), 1042-S form (Step 22), W-8BEN, payment records · retained through Mar 2032 per IRS §6501 7-year retention
IRS §6501 · Art. 17(3)(b)
Fraud markers 14 records
SB-2026-0083 (fake ID), associated device/IP hashes, network-graph node ID, FA-2026-0042 cluster link · retained for open investigation + 3 years post-closure
FA-2026-0042 · Art. 17(3)(e)
Audit logs 98 records
Immutable admin/system action log involving Marek's account · cannot delete per Atlas immutable-audit policy · 7-year retention
GDPR Art. 30 · Art. 17(3)(b)
Breach scope identifier 1 record
SI-2026-0014 breach-scope tag · required for ICO follow-up + 72h notification record · retained for breach lifecycle + 3 years
SI-2026-0014 · Art. 17(3)(b)
Suspension & appeal record 23 records
SB-2026-0083 suspension order, appeal correspondence, evidence package · retained until appeal resolved + 3 years for legal-claims defense
SB-2026-0083 · Art. 17(3)(e)
Ready to execute partial deletion. 7 categories · approx 4,200 records will be permanently deleted. 5 categories · approx 280 records will be retained with legal-basis tags. This action is irreversible for the deletion side. Audit-logged. Subject will be notified per Art. 12(3).
03

Action timeline

chronological log · 30-day SLA clock started Apr 28 · all actions audit-logged · Article 30 record-of-processing
May 9 · 10:30 UTC Cross-team retention review complete by Aïsha Okafor (DPO)
Trust & Safety confirms Marek's data needed for FA-2026-0042 ring analysis (open). Tax team confirms 1042-S retention through 2032. Engineering confirms scope preview accuracy. Awaiting Dario sign-off for partial-deletion recommendation.
May 7 · 16:45 UTC Legal hold reviewed · Art. 17(3) exemptions applicable by Aïsha Okafor (DPO)
DPO confirms 3 strong Art. 17(3) exemptions: (b) tax law obligation, (b) Art. 30 record-keeping, (e) defense of legal claims (fraud + suspension appeal). 1 weak supporting ground: (c) public interest. Full deletion NOT possible; partial deletion proceeds.
May 5 · 11:20 UTC Article 17 grounds analysis initiated by Aïsha Okafor (DPO)
Cross-referenced data inventory against retention obligations. Identified competing legal grounds. Drafted scope preview with delete/retain split. Sent to T&S, Tax, and Compliance for sign-off.
May 3 · 14:10 UTC DPO assigned · legal review begins by Dario Fonseca
Routed to Aïsha as Data Protection Officer due to deletion-type request + presence of fraud markers. Article 17(3) flag set in DSR-system. SLA paused for legal review window (15 days max).
May 1 · 09:32 UTC Identity verified · 3 methods · auto-confirmed by System
SMS OTP + email link + suspended-account portal 2FA all confirmed within 4-minute window. Same IP, known device, 100% match with prior session history. Risk score 0.08 (low). Gate unlocked.
Apr 29 · 09:15 UTC Auto-acknowledgement sent · 30-day SLA started by System
Confirmation email sent to subject. Internal routing to DPO queue. SLA clock started: 30 days from Apr 28 = May 28 deadline. Article 12(3) notification window opened.
Apr 28 · 14:22 UTC Deletion request filed via standard DSR form by Marek Kowalczyk (cand-1142)
Subject filed via /privacy/data-rights form. Stated grounds: Article 17(1)(a) — "data no longer necessary after account suspension". Request scope: "full erasure of all personal data including suspended account".
04

Internal DPO notes

privileged compliance work product · admin-only · NOT shared with subject · audit-logged
AO
Aïsha Okafor DPO · matter lead May 9 · 11:00 UTC
Final recommendation: partial deletion with 5 retention categories. The financial-records ground is unimpeachable (IRS law). The fraud-defense ground is strong while FA-2026-0042 is open and SB-2026-0083 is under appeal. We document Art. 30 carefully — ICO will likely review given the prior breach (SI-2026-0014). Need Dario sign-off before execution. Notification to Marek will cite specific articles and reference his appeal rights.
DF
Dario Fonseca Super Admin May 7 · 17:30 UTC
Agreed with DPO's framing. The appeal to SB-2026-0083 is still active; if appeal succeeds and account is reinstated, we'd need profile data back — argues against full deletion. Even with partial, we should preserve appeal-related data for defense. T&S advises against deleting fraud markers until FA-2026-0042 closes. Path forward: partial deletion + clear notification + retain hooks for appeal outcome.
AO
Add privileged note as Aïsha You · DPO
Internal · privileged · Art. 30 record
RESPONSE DEADLINE · GDPR ART. 12(3)
